question.text: Under Rule 8 of the PML Rules, Immovable Property Reports (IPRs) must be furnished to FIU-IND:

answer.text: Monthly, by the 15th day of the succeeding month
Within 7 working days of the transaction
Quarterly, by the 15th day of the month succeeding the quarter
Annually, within 30 days of the end of the financial year

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Rule 8(3) requires the Principal Officer to furnish information on immovable property transactions (Rule 3(F)) every quarter to the Director by the 15th day of the month succeeding the quarter. This is different from CTRs/CBWTRs (monthly) and STRs (7 working days).
**The Trap:** Three different timelines exist: Monthly by 15th (CTR, CBWTR, NPO, forged currency), Quarterly by 15th (IPR), and 7 working days (STR). Candidates frequently mix up IPR with the monthly reports.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Series XXIV tests the same quarterly IPR reporting. The IFSCA exam tests all three timelines to ensure candidates can distinguish between them.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** IPR reporting is quarterly because immovable property transactions are less frequent than cash or wire transfers. But the 15th-day deadline from the end of the quarter is strict -- each day of delay is a separate violation.
